modules.title: Technical Communication Skills
topics: Understanding the process and scope of communication
Relevance and importance of communication in a globalized world
Role of unity, brevity and clarity in various forms of communication

modules.title: Types of Communication and Soft Skills
Writing Skills and Speaking Skills
topics: Types, characteristics, format and structure of technical reports
Speaking to one and to one thousand listeners

modules.title: Job Interviews, Group Discussion and Professional Presentations
topics: Method, focus, content, style and argumentation skills in group discussion
Individual professional presentations: audience awareness, body language, delivery and content

modules.title: Grammar and Linguistic Ability
topics: Common errors in writing and speaking
Tone modulation: rising tone and falling tone

reference_books: Sharon Gerson and Steven Gerson. Technical Writing: Process and Product (8th Edition), London: Longman, 2013
Rentz, Kathryn, Marie E. Flatley & Paula Lentz. Lesikar’s Business Communication Connecting in a Digital world, McGraw-Hill, Irwin. 2012
Allan & Barbara Pease. The Definitive Book of Body Language, New York, Bantam, 2004
Jones, Daniel. The Pronunciation of English, New Delhi, Universal Book Stall. 2010
Sharma, Sangeeta & Mishra, Binod. Communication Skills for Engineers and Scientists, New Delhi: PHI Learning, 2009, rpt 2012

subjects.subject_name: Probability and Statistics for Data Science

modules.title: Data Science and Statistics Fundamentals
topics: Data Science: Introduction and Data Science Life Cycle
Measures of central tendency: Arithmetic Mean, Median, Mode, Geometric Mean, Harmonic Mean, Partition values
Measures of dispersion: Range, Quartile Deviation, Mean deviation, Standard Deviation, Variance, Coefficient of Dispersion

modules.title: Probability Theory and Distribution Shape Measures
topics: Skewness, Kurtosis, Moments; measures of skewness and kurtosis
Theory of probability: definition of probability, events, sample space, laws of addition and multiplication, conditional probability
Independent and dependent events, Bayes’ theorem, mathematical expectations, moment generating functions

modules.title: Theoretical Distributions and Curve Fitting
topics: Discrete distributions: Binomial distribution and Poisson distribution
Continuous distributions: Rectangular (uniform) distribution and Normal distribution
Curve fitting: Methods of least squares, fitting a straight line and a parabola

modules.title: Correlation and Regression Analysis
topics: Correlation: Pearson correlation coefficient, rank correlation, lines of regression

modules.title: Hypothesis Testing and Significance Tests
topics: Testing of hypothesis: null and alternative hypotheses, Type I and Type II errors, level of significance, power of the test
Tests of significance: chi‑square distribution, tests of variance and goodness‑of‑fit, t‑distribution, F‑distribution, Z‑distribution and related tests

reference_books: S.C. Gupta, V.K. Kapoor – “Fundamentals of Mathematical Statistics”, 10th Edition, Sultan Chand, 2000 (ISBN 8170147913, 9788170147916)
D.N. Elhance – ‘Fundamentals of Mathematical Statistics’, Kitab Mahal, Allahabad
A.M. Goon, M.K. Gupta & B. Dasgupta – An Outline of Statistical Theory, Vol. I, 6th revised edition, World Press (1980)

modules.title: Introduction to Data Structures
topics: Cost estimation of data structure operations
Applications of linked list (e.g., polynomial manipulation)

modules.title: Stacks and Queues
topics: Applications of stack – infix to postfix conversion, postfix evaluation, recursion

modules.title: Tree Data Structures
topics: Tree definitions – height, depth, order, degree
Binary Search Tree – operations, traversal, search

modules.title: Graphs
topics: Graph definitions and classification – directed and undirected graphs
Graph traversal – Depth‑First Search (DFS), Breadth‑First Search (BFS)
Minimum Spanning Tree algorithms – Kruskal, Prim

modules.title: Sorting, Searching and Hashing
topics: Sorting techniques – Bubble sort, Quick sort, Selection sort, Heap sort, Insertion sort, Shell sort, Merge sort, Radix sort
Searching techniques – Sequential search, Binary search
Case studies – applications in operating systems, DBMS

reference_books: Aho, Hopcroft, Ullman, “Data Structures and Algorithms”, Pearson Education.
N. Wirth, “Algorithms + Data Structure = Programs”, Prentice Hall.
Jean‑Paul Tremblay, Paul Sorenson, “An Introduction to Data Structure with application”, TMH.
Richard, Gilberg, Behrouz, Forouzan, “Data structure – A Pseudocode Approach with C”, Thomson press.

modules.title: Fundamentals of Artificial Intelligence
topics: History, motivation and need of AI
Search space and search techniques: hill climbing, best‑first search, heuristic search, A* and AO*

modules.title: Knowledge Representation
topics: Comparison of propositional and predicate logic

modules.title: Probabilistic Reasoning and Semantic Networks
topics: Semantic networks, scripts, schemas and frames

modules.title: Game Playing, Planning and Natural Language Processing
topics: Game playing techniques: minimax procedure and alpha‑beta cut‑offs
Planning and the block‑world problem in robotics
Components of NLP and its application to expert systems

modules.title: Expert Systems
topics: Characteristics, requirements and components of expert systems
Inference engine: forward and backward chaining
Expert system development environment, technology and benefits

reference_books: Janakiraman, K. Sarukesi, ‘Foundations of Artificial Intelligence and Expert Systems’, Macmillan Series in Computer Science.
W. Patterson, ‘Introduction to Artificial Intelligence and Expert Systems’, Prentice Hall of India.

modules.title: Introduction to Object Oriented Thinking & Programming
topics: Features of Object Oriented paradigm – merits and demerits

modules.title: Encapsulation and Data Abstraction
topics: Concept of Objects: state, behavior & identity

modules.title: Advanced Topics
topics: Case studies: ATM system, Library management system

reference_books: G. Booch, “Object Oriented Analysis & Design”, Addison Wesley.
James Martin, “Principles of Object Oriented Analysis and Design”, Prentice Hall/PTR.
Peter Coad and Edward Yourdon, “Object Oriented Design”, Prentice Hall/PTR.
Herbert Schildt, “Java 2: The Complete Reference”, McGraw-Hill Osborne Media.

experiments: To write a Python program to find GCD of two numbers.
To write a Python Program to find the square root of a number by Newton’s Method.
To write a Python program to find the exponentiation of a number.
To write a Python Program to find the maximum from a list of numbers.
To write a Python Program to perform Linear Search.
To write a Python Program to perform binary search.
To write a Python Program to perform selection sort.
To write a Python Program to perform insertion sort.
To write a Python Program to perform Merge sort.
To write a Python program to find first n prime numbers.
To write a Python program to multiply matrices.
To write a Python program for command line arguments.
To write a Python program to find the most frequent words in a text read from a file.
To write a Python program to simulate elliptical orbits in Pygame.
To write a Python program to bouncing ball in Pygame.
reference_books: Timothy A. Budd: Exploring python, McGraw-Hill Education.
R.Nageshwar Rao, "Python Programming", Wiley India.
Think Python: Allen B. Downey, O'Reilly Media, Inc.
